markdownRemark.excerpt: React Context API  is a way to declare a global state to make it available for multiple components. The main reason this is used is to avoid…
markdownRemark.html:
React Context API is a way to declare a global state to make it available for multiple components. The main reason this is used is to avoid what’s known as prop drilling, which means if we have a Grandparent (GP), Parent (P) and a Grandchild (GC), we normally would have to send information from GP->P->GC, so what’s wrong about this approach?

  1. We write more code because we would need to declare the props inside propTypes (it lets other devs know the type of data the props are).
  2. For a highly reusable component, adding too many props can overload it making it messy codewise.
  3. What happens if we instead of going through one parent, it goes through four? Yes, you add unnecesary code.

So in conclusion, use React Context to avoid prop drilling.

Is React Context an alternative to Redux?

It depends on what you’re trying to accomplish, if its communicating simple values from child to parent or between siblings, you can use Context with callback functions (we will see this in another post) to communicate from child to parent or if its between siblings, you use it normally.

But Redux has a lot of advantages which include less code overall because you follow the DRY principle. If we don’t use Redux in an app where we have to do a CRUD, we would need to use local state to know when the app is loading, when it gives an error or when its successful, do you imagine repeating this for 10 components? When we can just import an Action Creator that let us do the simple fetch and let the reducer handle the loading, error and successful events.

In conclusion, its not an alternative to Redux in cases where we need Redux. Use it mostly to avoid prop drilling.

Grandparent to grandchild

For a quick example we create a context.js file which we use to create our context and add an initial value (similar to state), we export the Provider of the value and Context (used by the GC).

In our GP component we import the Provider and surround the component, then we send the information we want to the grandchild through the value property

Now we can ignore the parent and go to the GC, we import the Context from our context.js file. We use “useContext” to get the value from our GP and use it however we want! We avoid prop drilling and declaring propTypes as well for parent

Grandchild to Grandparent

We will use the callback functions to communicate between Grandchild and Grandparent using React Context.

Conclusion

See you on the next post.

Sincerely,

Eng Adrian Beria.

frontmatter.title: Third Project - Redux - Part 2 Toolbar and CRUD
